contentSections.statement: You work at MyFund&Stocks, a platform that lets investors track their investments in equity mutual funds.

Equity mutual funds are a collection of individual stocks. Fund overlap is the term used to quantify the percentage of common stocks between different mutual funds. Investors prefer to have funds with as low an overlap as possible, since investing in different mutual funds with a high overlap will not give the benefits of diversification.
Available mutual funds and their respective stocks are mentioned here.

contentSections.heading: Portfolio Overlap
contentSections.statement: Overlap is defined as a measure of the commonality between portfolios.

Suppose there are 2 funds A & B. The overlap between them is given as the number of stocks in common, counting both portfolio A and B( (i.e. double counting the common elements) divided by the total number of elements in portfolio A and portfolio B.

contentSections.heading: Overlap (A,B) =
contentSections.statement: 2*(No of common stocks in A & B)/ (No of stocks in A + No of stocks in B) * 100
Your program should take as input:
orderedList: Name of current funds the user holds in his/her portfolio.
Stock to be added for existing fund.
Overlapping stocks of the new fund with the current funds.
If a given fund name is not present while calculating overlap or adding stocks to existing fund, the output should be FUND_NOT_FOUND.

contentSections.heading: Input Commands
contentSections.statement: There are 4 input commands defined to separate out the actions. Your input format will start with either of these commands i.e CURRENT_PORTFOLIO, CALCULATE_OVERLAP, OVERLAP_PERCENTAGE, ADD_STOCK

subStatements.subStatement: The CURRENT_PORTFOLIO command receives the current funds that the user holds in his/her portfolio.
subStatements.subStatementExamples:
Format - CURRENT_PORTFOLIO FUND_NAME_1 FUND_NAME_2

Example- CURRENT_PORTFOLIO AXIS_BLUECHIP ICICI_PRU_BLUECHIP means that the current fund that the user holds in his/her portfolio are AXIS_BLUECHIP and ICICI_PRU_BLUECHIP

subStatements.subStatement: The CALCULATE_OVERLAP command receives the fund name and calculates the overlap between that fund with each fund in the current portfolio.
subStatements.subStatementExamples:
Format - CALCULATE_OVERLAP FUND_NAME

Example - CALCULATE_OVERLAP AXIS_BLUECHIP means calculate the percentage of overlapping stocks of AXIS_BLUECHIP with each fund that the user holds in his/her current portfolio.

subStatements.subStatement: The ADD_STOCK command receives the fund name to which the new stock will be added and the name of the new stock.
subStatements.subStatementExamples:
Format - ADD_STOCK FUND_NAME STOCK_NAME

Example - ADD_STOCK AXIS_BLUECHIP HDFC BANK LIMITED means we have to add the stock HDFC BANK LIMITED to AXIS_BLUECHIP fund.

contentSections.heading: Assumptions
orderedList: All the input funds will be those that are present in the available funds json provided.
The overlap percentages will be rounded to 2 decimal points
The stocks names can have spaces in between, for eg: HDFC BANK LIMITED

CALCULATE_OVERLAP MIRAE_ASSET_EMERGING_BLUECHIP
